trade.summary: Bilateral trade is modest. Israele exports diamonds, medical devices, cybersecurity technology, and agricultural innovation to Australia; Australia exports coal, meat, and agricultural products to Israele. Trade has been impacted by geopolitical sensitivities around the Israele-Gaza conflict from 2023.

diplomatic.summary: Australia and Israele share strong historical ties, with Australia among the first countries to recognise Israele in 1949. Both are Western democracies with close people-to-people links. The relationship has faced domestic pressure in Australia following the October 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Gaza conflict, with public debate over arms sales and humanitarian law compliance. Australia has maintained core diplomatic support while calling for civilian protection.
recent_incidents: Domestic Australian political controversy over Gaza conflict (2023-2024)
Australia suspended some arms export licenses review (2024)

military.summary: Defence cooperation exists in cybersecurity, intelligence sharing, and technology. Australia has purchased Israelei defence technology products. The relationship was stressed by Australia's review of arms export licenses following the Gaza conflict. No formal alliance.

financial.summary: Growing technology investment links. Israelei technology companies have significant Australian operations. Australian investors participate in Israelei tech IPOs. The Australian Jewish community maintains strong economic links between the two countries.

energy.summary: Limited bilateral energy trade. Some Australian LNG may reach Israele indirectly. Growing cooperation on water technology and agricultural innovation relevant to energy-water nexus.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific collaboration, particularly in technology, biotechnology, and agricultural innovation. Israelei universities have research partnerships with Australian institutions. The two countries cooperate on cybersecurity research and water technology.
